creator: netchild@FreeBSD.org
text:
The port takes what is delivered by the vendor. It is not compiled on FreeBSD, it is the vendor supplied distribution/code/versions. The vendor doesn't support FreeBSD itself, so it depends what is in the official jar which comes with Sonarqube.

Sonarqube should work without jna. At least this was the case in the past. If you look at the makefile of the sonarqube-community port, you will see that I have some provisions of using jna from ports there. I had it active during a short timeframe because I have seen a failure of loading the jna stuff on amd64 too. At some point I noticed issues after an upgrade, and commented it out, so that the jna stuff wasn't loaded anymore. My sonarqube instance still worked.

It seems at some point sonarqube shipped a jna jar which includes now the FreeBSD amd64 and i386 architectures. As such my instance on amd64 doesn't show the jna exception anymore.

If arm64 has a working jna package, you could install it and try:
  rm /usr/local/libexec/sonarqube/elasticsearch/lib/jna-5.10.0.jar
  ln -s /usr/local/share/java/classes/jna.jar \
               /usr/local/libexec/sonarqube/elasticsearch/lib/jna.jar

If there is no change in elasticsearch which makes the missing jna stuff fatal, sonarqube should work without jna. But if the missing jna stuff is now fatal, I would have to mark the port for amd64 and i386 only or check the use of the jna port/package again.
